2026-04-07T20:35:21.0920634Z 
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0921444Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, repo: azure-sdk-for-net, path: sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Cor...)
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0922262Z 
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0923089Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, repo: azure-sdk-for-net, path: sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Cor...)
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0923872Z 
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0924663Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, path: sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Cor..., repo: azure-sdk-for-net)
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0925418Z 
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0926211Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, path: sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Cor..., repo: azure-sdk-for-net)
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0927006Z 
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0927336Z ◆ Now I have a good picture. Let me analyze:
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0927781Z   
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0929606Z   1. The commit moved `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` from `OnStart` to `OnEnd` in `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` to prevent the OpenAI SDK from overwriting them during span lifetime.
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0931093Z   
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0932110Z   2. The README has a **Telemetry** section under Key Concepts that mentions `FoundryEnvironment` and describes OpenTelemetry setup, but it does NOT describe:
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0933628Z      - The `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` itself or its behavior
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0934806Z      - The specific OpenTelemetry span tags set (`gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, `gen_ai.agent.id`, `microsoft.foundry.project.id`)
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0936198Z      - How the processor uses tag precedence to override framework-stamped values at span end
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0937439Z      - The environment variables that drive this (`FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME`, `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION`, `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID`)
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0938519Z      - How `gen_ai.agent.id` is derived from agent name and version
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0939313Z   
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0939930Z   3. The CHANGELOG doesn't document this behavioral shift from `OnStart` to `OnEnd` placement.
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0940690Z   
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0941834Z   4. The README references `AgentHostTelemetry` and `ResponsesSourceName` but omits any explanation of `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` and the span attributes it actually sets.
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0942958Z   
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0943866Z   5. The README covers all major sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing), though Examples is sparse.
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0944894Z   
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0946904Z   There's a clear documentation gap here: the Telemetry section doesn't explain what attributes `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` adds, the CHANGELOG skips this behavioral change, and there's no mention of the `OnEnd` override mechanism that users would need to understand when debugging span values. I should file an issue for this.
2026-04-07T20:35:21.0949335Z

2026-04-07T20:35:21.0953913Z

Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. 2026-04-07T20:35:21.2931071Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T20:34:21.186557994Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System.Diagnostics;\nusing OpenTelemetry;\n\nnamespace Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal;\n\n/// \u003csummary\u003e\n/// An OpenTelemetry span processor that enriches every span with Foundry\n/// agent identity and project attributes. This processor is automatically\n/// registered by \u003csee cref=\"OpenTelemetryExtensions.AddAgentHostTelemetry\"/\u003e\n/// so that all protocol packages receive the enrichment without additional\n/// configuration.\n/// \u003c/summary\u003e\n/// \u003cremarks\u003e\n/// The processor reads \u003cc\u003eFOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME\u003c/c\u003e, \u003cc\u003eFOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION\u003c/c\u003e,\n/// and \u003cc\u003eFOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID\u003c/c\u003e once at construction time and stamps\n/// them onto every started \u003csee cref=\"Activity\"/\u003e.\n/// \u003c/remarks\u003e\ninternal sealed class FoundryEnrichmentProcessor : BaseProcessor\u003cActivity\u003e\n{\n    private readonly string? _agentName;\n    private readonly string? _agentVersion;\n    private readonly string? _agentId;\n    private readonly string? _projectId;\n\n    public FoundryEnrichmentProcessor()\n    {\n        _agentName = FoundryEnvironment.AgentName;\n        _agentVersion = FoundryEnvironment.AgentVersion;\n        _projectId = FoundryEnvironment.ProjectArmId;\n\n        _agentId = _agentName is not null \u0026\u0026 _agentVersion is not null\n            ? $\"{_agentName}:{_agentVersion}\"\n            : _agentName;\n    }\n\n    /// \u003cinheritdoc/\u003e\n    public override void OnStart(Activity activity)\n    {\n        if (_projectId is not null)\n        {\n            activity.SetTag(\"microsoft.foundry.project.id\", _projectId);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// \u003cinheritdoc/\u003e\n    /// \u003cremarks\u003e\n    /// Agent identity tags are set in \u003cc\u003eOnEnd\u003c/c\u003e rather than \u003cc\u003eOnStart\u003c/c\u003e\n    /// so that they take precedence over any values an underlying framework\n    /// (e.g. the OpenAI SDK) may have stamped during the span's lifetime.\n    /// \u003c/remarks\u003e\n    public override void OnEnd(Activity activity)\n    {\n        if (_agentName is not null)\n        {\n            activity.SetTag(\"gen_ai.agent.name\", _agentName);\n        }\n\n        if (_agentVersion is not null)\n        {\n            activity.SetTag(\"gen_ai.agent.version\", _agentVersion);\n        }\n\n        if (_agentId is not null)\n        {\n            activity.SetTag(\"gen_ai.agent.id\", _agentId);\n        }\n    }\n}\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/src/Telemetry/FoundryEnrichmentProcessor.cs"},"type":"resource"}]}}}

2026-04-07T20:35:21.2971749Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T20:34:26.851810636Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Release History\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.21 (Unreleased)\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n- Package renamed from `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Hosting` to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`.\n- Namespace changed from `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Hosting` to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`.\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Library-owned hosting foundation via `AgentHostBuilder` (composable builder pattern).\n- OpenTelemetry integration with `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore` and OTLP exporter support.\n- Health endpoint at `/readiness` for liveness and readiness probes.\n- Multi-protocol composition via `AgentHostBuilder.RegisterProtocol()`. Protocol packages provide extension methods (e.g., `AddResponses\u003cT\u003e()`, `AddInvocations\u003cT\u003e()`) built on top of this API.\n- Graceful shutdown with configurable drain period.\n- Server user-agent `x-platform-server` header on every response with SDK version info.\n- `ServerUserAgentRegistry` for protocol packages to register user-agent identity segments.\n- `AddAgentServerUserAgent()` and `UseAgentServerUserAgent()` extensions for standalone (Tier 3) setups.\n- `FoundryEnvironment` for Azure AI Foundry platform variable resolution.\n- Distributed tracing context propagation via request ID baggage.\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/CHANGELOG.md"},"type":"resource"}]}}}
2026-04-07T20:35:21.2996212Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T20:34:26.877784249Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Azure AI Agent Server Core library for .NET\n\nAzure.AI.AgentServer.Core is a shared hosting foundation for Azure AI Agent Server packages. It provides a library-owned ASP.NET Core host with built-in OpenTelemetry, health checks, graceful shutdown, and multi-protocol composition — so you can go from `dotnet add package` to a running agent server in minutes.\n\n[Source code][source] | [Package (NuGet)][nuget] | [Product documentation][product_doc]\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Install the package\n\nInstall the library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/):\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core --prerelease\n```\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n- An [Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/)\n- [.NET 8](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) or later\n\n### Start a server (recommended)\n\nUse the builder pattern to create a server. Protocol packages provide extension methods (`AddResponses\u003cT\u003e()`, `AddInvocations\u003cT\u003e()`) to register their endpoints:\n\n```C# Snippet:Core_ReadMe_CreateBuilder\nvar builder = AgentHost.CreateBuilder();\n\n// Register protocol endpoints (protocol packages provide extension methods).\nbuilder.RegisterProtocol(\"MyProtocol\", endpoints =\u003e\n{\n    endpoints.MapGet(\"/hello\", () =\u003e \"Hello from the agent server!\");\n});\n\nvar app = builder.Build();\napp.Run();\n```\n\nThis starts a Kestrel server with OpenTelemetry, a `/readiness` health endpoint, and the `x-platform-server` identity header.\n\n## Key concepts\n\n### AgentHost\n\nThe static entry point. `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` returns an `AgentHostBuilder` for composing protocols and configuring the server.\n\n### AgentHostBuilder\n\nConfigures the underlying ASP.NET Core host with sensible defaults: Kestrel on the `PORT` environment variable (or 8088), OpenTelemetry traces and metrics, a `/readiness` health endpoint, and `x-platform-server` user-agent header. Protocol packages use `RegisterProtocol()` to add their endpoints — each protocol registers its identity segment with the `ServerUserAgentRegistry`.\n\n### AgentHostApp\n\nThe built application. Call `Run()` to start listening. Wraps `WebApplication` with server-specific configuration applied.\n\n### FoundryEnvironment\n\nReads Azure AI Foundry platform variables (`FOUNDRY_*`, `PORT`, `SSE_KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL`) to resolve agent identity, listening port, and connection strings. Also detects `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` and `APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING` for telemetry configuration. Useful when your agent server runs as a hosted agent in AI Foundry.\n\n### Telemetry\n\nOpenTelemetry is configured automatically via `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore`. The `AgentHostTelemetry` class exposes the shared activity source and meter names (`ResponsesSourceName`, `InvocationsSourceName`, `ResponsesMeterName`, `InvocationsMeterName`) for the Responses and Invocations protocols. OTLP export is enabled when the `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` environment variable is set.\n\n### Health endpoint\n\nA `/readiness` endpoint is registered by default, responding to liveness and readiness probes. It reports healthy as soon as the host finishes starting.\n\n## Examples\n\nYou can familiarise yourself with different APIs using [Samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/samples).\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Common errors\n\n- **Port already in use**: The server defaults to port 8088 (or the `PORT` environment variable). If the port is occupied, set `PORT` to another value or configure Kestrel directly via the builder.\n- **No protocol registered**: If you use `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` without calling `RegisterProtocol()` (or a protocol extension method), the server will start but will have no protocol endpoints mapped.\n\n### Logging\n\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to diagnose startup issues.\n\n## Next steps\n\n- [Samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/samples) — Getting started, multi-protocol composition\n\n## Cont
